name: Green Olive at Red Hill
signature: A working olive grove with a produce-driven kitchen — grazing boards, wood-fired mains, and a seasonal long lunch under the trees
editorNote:
Green Olive is a working olive grove and smallholding on the ridge, and the on-site kitchen has become one of the more underrated long-lunch destinations in Red Hill. The menu is produce-driven in a genuine way — the olives and oils come from the grove itself, the vegetables are worked out of the kitchen garden, and the wood oven runs through most of the mains. Grazing boards for the middle of the day; slow-cooked lamb and whole fish for a proper sit-down.

The setting is the other reason to come. The dining room spills out into the grove, and on a spring lunch with the trees shaded and the oil tasting still on the table, it feels as close to a Mediterranean smallholding as the Peninsula gets.

Go for a long lunch on a sunny Sunday. Buy a bottle of the estate oil on the way out.
